primary_question: Is the price of time helping or hurting long-duration assets?

cache.source_policy: Compact seed cache for UI verification when live FRED fetch is unavailable in CI. Replace with refreshed FRED CSV cache before production-grade analysis.

doctrine.primary_rule: Render raw duration data first; annotations and interpretation are overlays.
doctrine.purpose: Expose whether nominal yields, real yields, and curve shape support or constrain long-duration assets.

macro_gate_contribution.caveat: Mission 5A covers Treasury curve, nominal yield, real yield, and breakeven structure. Term premium, issuance, fiscal pressure, MOVE, and TLT remain missing evidence.

duration_main.title: 10Y Treasury yield vs 10Y real yield vs 10Y breakeven

web_summary.lead: Duration shows whether time is becoming cheaper or more expensive for long-duration assets.

analysis.statement: High nominal and real yields keep duration restrictive. Falling real yields and healthier curve structure would improve duration support.
analysis.invalidation: Do not upgrade duration support without improving real yields, curve structure, and volatility confirmation.

derivation_notes: 2s10s and 3m10y are carry-forward aligned spread series.
limitations: Compact seed cache is for visual verification only.
Live FRED refresh should replace this cache before production-grade analysis.
Term premium, Treasury issuance pressure, fiscal deficit pressure, MOVE index, and TLT price trend remain missing evidence in Mission 5A.
generator.annotation_policy: Annotate supported Duration set points; suppress unsupported points rather than inventing values.

annotation_spec.policy: Annotate generously only when the chart dataset covers the event or regime window. If the series does not reach the set point, suppress the annotation and record the omission.

duration_main.label: 1979-1982 Volcker tightening
duration_main.why_it_matters: Reference for high real rates, inflation suppression, valuation compression, and cash competing with risk.

duration_main.label: 1994 bond shock
duration_main.why_it_matters: Reference for surprise tightening and duration repricing without a full economic collapse.

duration_main.label: 2007-2009 global financial crisis
duration_main.why_it_matters: Reference for credit collapse, liquidity demand, duration safety, equity drawdown, and policy rescue.

duration_main.label: 2009-2015 QE / low-rate repair regime
duration_main.why_it_matters: Reference for low cash return, liquidity support, financial repression, and long-duration asset support.

duration_main.label: 2018 late-cycle tightening / Q4 risk shock
duration_main.why_it_matters: Reference for late-cycle tightening, balance-sheet runoff sensitivity, and valuation stress.

duration_main.label: 2020 COVID liquidity shock
duration_main.why_it_matters: Reference for sudden stop, emergency easing, liquidity flood, and cross-asset dislocation.

duration_main.label: 2022 inflation / hiking shock
duration_main.why_it_matters: Reference for rate shock, long-duration valuation reset, dollar strength, and cash yield revival.

duration_main.label: 2023 regional-bank stress
duration_main.why_it_matters: Reference for deposit flight, bank balance-sheet duration stress, and policy liquidity backstop.

duration_main.label: 2023-present AI infrastructure capex / market concentration cycle
duration_main.why_it_matters: Reference for current cycle: capital concentration, AI infrastructure buildout, capex intensity, valuation expectations, and uncertain productivity payback.

duration_main.label: 2016-2021 cloud / SaaS multiple expansion
duration_main.why_it_matters: Analog for low-rate support of recurring-revenue long-duration equities and subsequent sensitivity to rate repricing.
